creator: portmaster@bsdforge.com
text:
Greetings, and thank you for the reply.
> +--On 20 janvier 2014 19:10:01 +0000 C Hutchinson <portmaster@bsdforge.com>
> wrote:
> |  > Plus, NO_STAGE is not
> |  > a variable you can set, it's going to wreck a lot of things.
> |
> |  Has not been my experience. In fact, quite the opposite. As to:
> |  NO_STAGE is not a variable you can set...
> |
> |  Which is where I found out about it, when I began experiencing issues
> | w/it.  There are other (more recent) remarks regarding NO_STAGE, as well.
>
> Ok, I get it. So the entry wasn't clear enough. There is only *one* usage
> of NO_STAGE, and it is of being added to a port *once* at the start. Then
> it gets converted to staging, and you can't, ever, expect to have NO_STAGE
> do anything clever again.

Interesting. But I can't help but notice, that however you want to slice the
pie; -DNO_STAGE was the ONLY change I made to a standard make install. It was
the ONLY difference, and for whatever reason, allowed install w/o error.

>
> |  > I can't reproduce your problem with a new, plain, vanilla setup on my
> | dev  > box, and it also builds fine in poudriere, which is used to
> | generate our  > packages.
> |
> |  Interesting. The box I'm building, is intended to become a
> |  Pointyhat/Tinderbox. It's the first fresh install I've performed in
> | years,  as all of my other servers have been tracking -STABLE for the
> | past many  years. This install comes from the 8.4-dvd1 DVD. Immediately
> | after the  install, I bounced the box, deleted /usr/src && /usr/ports.
> | Performed  portsnap fetch && portsnap extract. Cobbled a new kernel.
> | Built, and  installed world/kernel. Then began building/installing
> | requisite ports.  Failing on this one. I should also add, I have no
> | make.conf(5). src.conf(5)  contains only WITHOUT_CLANG=true. Deleting
> | src.conf(5) had no affect.  In summary; fresh install of 8.4-STABLE, src,
> | and ports r260592
>
> Well, it's strange, because it builds just fine there:
>
> <http://beefy1.isc.freebsd.org/bulk/83i386-default/latest-per-pkg/yadifa-1.0.3_1.log>
> <http://beefy2.isc.freebsd.org/bulk/83amd64-default/latest-per-pkg/yadifa-1.0.3_1.log>
> <http://pkg.absolight.net/logs/bulk/83amd64-pkgng/latest-per-pkg/yadifa-1.0.3_1.log>
> <http://pkg.absolight.net/logs/bulk/83i386-pkgng/latest-per-pkg/yadifa-1.0.3_1.log>

Indeed. Quite odd.

>
> |  (2014-01-13), on an i386.
> |  The ONLY way I could successfully install dns/yadifa, was after appending
> |  -DNO_STAGE to make, during both make && install.
> |  I also saw PR 185897, had similar, or same issue, back in November. How
> |  was this solved?
>
> It was not, I still can't reproduce it, and I can't fix what I can't
> reproduce.

Sure. So what must I do, other than what I have already provided, to make
the difference?

>
> Hum, then it occurs to me, are you still using the deprecated and soon to
> be removed pkg_install ?

Dunno. Not intentionally. Like I said; a /standard/ install from dvd1,
followed by a standard portsnap(8), which provided a CLEAN/DEFAULT copy
of src, and ports. Pretty "vanilla", wouldn't you say? I haven't setup, or
used pkg_install. So unless DEFAULT /insists/ on using it. The answer is no.

>
> --
> Mathieu Arnold

Hope this helps, and thanks again, for the response.

--Chris

>
